Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}Ondate 4 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 44.773 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =7|経度秒 = 23.131 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Washitori-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii of Washitori Shrine]] '''Washitori Shrine''' (わしとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わしとりしんしや おかさきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡崎市の神社]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わしとりしんしや おかさきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡崎市の神社]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}Ondate 4 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 44.773 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =7|経度秒 = 23.131 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Washitori-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii of Washitori Shrine]] '''Washitori Shrine''' (わしとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡崎市の神社]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}Ondate 4 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 44.773 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =7|経度秒 = 23.131 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Washitori-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii of Washitori Shrine]] '''Washitori Shrine''' (わしとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}Ondate 4 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 44.773 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =7|経度秒 = 23.131 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Washitori-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii of Washitori Shrine]] '''Washitori Shrine''' (わしとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nishihongō-chō (Okazaki)|Q132044357|WD=Q132044357|lt=Nishihongō-chō|1=Nishihongō-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]]

It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of Okazaki Tourism Kirari 100 Selections]. === Overview === There are unclear points about its founding, but it is regarded as one of the six ancient shrines of {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Founding is thought to date back to the Nara period, and a close relation with Ekiya-gō and Tottori relay station in Hekikai District is inferred. In the "Mikawa Kokunai Shinmei Meichō," said to have formed from late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Washitori Tenjin." The deity is {{ill|lt=Isasakiirihiko no Miko|Q18700560|WD=Q18700560|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}}. West of {{ill|lt=Renge-ji (Okazaki)|Q24873113|WD=Q24873113|lt=Renge-ji|1=Renge-ji}} in Nishihongō-chō is a kofun called {{ill|lt=Washiyama Kofun|Q28693043|WD=Q28693043|1=Washiyama Kofun}}, said to be the tomb of Isasakiirihiko no Miko. In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), it was renamed Hasebe Shrine. In May {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), by order of the {{ill|lt=Kyōbu-shō|Q4294653|WD=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, it was designated Shikinaisha Washitori Shrine, but this was canceled in February {{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), returning to the old name. In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), the shrine name Washitori Shrine was approved as an undecided-style shrine, and on December 28, {{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5), it was made gōsha. During early Meiji {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Daishi-dō in the precincts moved east. The wooden seated Dainichi Nyorai of Kongōkai and wooden standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu owned by Daishi-dō were designated in the Okazaki City designated cultural properties list on March 30, {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=Okazaki City designated cultural properties list | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. From Edo through mid-Meiji, this shrine's festival was known as "Omantō" or "Omanto" (meaning horse towers), and fireworks were customary. Every year, four major festivals are held: Kinen-sai, spring festival, annual festival, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In particular, at the autumn Niiname-sai, the sweet-sake festival celebrating good harvests has continued from {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}} (Taishō 4) to today. Pupils of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Municipal Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|Q11472901|WD=Q11472901|lt=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School|1=Yahagi Nishi Elementary School}} also continue the Urayasu no Mai dance. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Uto Station|Q7903518|WD=Q7903518|1=Utō Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}, about 1,100 m to the southeast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition, History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Edition History of Okazaki City Editorial Committee |page = 419-420 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Tour of Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 128-129 |isbn = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Washitori-jinja (Okazaki)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washitori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]]
